model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: UCLA holds a significant edge in both KenPom (#27 vs #54) and Torvik (#24 vs #58) rankings, with an adjusted efficiency margin roughly 6-8 points higher than UCF's, driven by advantages on both ends of the floor—particularly UCLA's elite offense (AdjO 123.8-124.5, 17th-22nd nationally) against UCF's below-average defense (AdjD 105.5-105.7, 114th nationally). The Bruins enter with strong momentum from their Big Ten Tournament run, beating Rutgers and Michigan State before narrowly falling to Purdue in the final, while UCF stumbled down the stretch with blowout losses to Oklahoma State and West Virginia. While UCF's Big 12 pedigree and quality wins over Kansas and Texas Tech give them a credible upset path in this historically competitive 7-10 seed line, the 5.5-point spread and UCLA's clear statistical superiority make the Bruins the deserving favorite.

reasoningStep.title: East 7/10: UCLA vs UCF
reasoningStep.summary: UCLA's superior efficiency profile on both offense and defense, combined with strong late-season form and a 5.5-point spread, give the Bruins a clear but not overwhelming edge over a UCF team that has quality Big 12 wins but struggled defensively and faded late in the season.
evidence: UCLA KenPom #27 (AdjEM +21.64, AdjO 123.8, AdjD 102.1) vs UCF KenPom #54 (AdjEM +15.05, AdjO 120.5, AdjD 105.5)
UCLA Torvik #24 (AdjOE 124.5, 17th; AdjDE 101.7, 49th) vs UCF Torvik #58 (AdjOE 120.0, 39th; AdjDE 105.7, 114th)
UCLA 23-11 in Big Ten with BTT wins over Rutgers, Michigan State; UCF 21-11 in Big 12 but lost 4 of last 6 including blowouts
7-10 seed matchups historically favor the 7-seed roughly 60-40, but UCF's Big 12 schedule and wins over Kansas and Texas Tech provide legitimate upset credibility

- Torvik: #24, AdjOE 124.5 (17th), AdjDE 101.7 (49th)
- Recent form: Won 4 of last 5 regular season games, then won 3 Big Ten Tournament games (beat Rutgers, Michigan State, then lost to Purdue in the final)
- Notable wins: Oregon, Purdue, Illinois, Michigan State (BTT)
- Notable losses: at Michigan (86-56), at Michigan State (82-59), at Iowa (74-61)

**UCF (10-seed, Big 12)**
- Record: 21-11
- KenPom: #54, AdjEM +15.05, AdjO 120.5 (40th), AdjD 105.5
- Torvik: #58, AdjOE 120.0 (39th), AdjDE 105.7 (114th)
- Notable wins: Kansas, Texas A&M, Texas Tech, BYU
- Notable losses: at Iowa State (87-57), at Houston (79-55), vs Arizona (81-59), at West Virginia (77-62)

The East Region game is scheduled for 4:25 p.m. MST and can be seen on TBS ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? modelTrace.content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me summarize the key findings:

**UCLA (7-seed)**
- KenPom #27, Torvik #24
- AdjEM: +21.64 (KenPom), ~+22.8 (Torvik)
- Elite offense: AdjO 123.8-124.5 (17th-22nd nationally)
- Solid defense: AdjD 101.7-102.1 (49th)
- Big Ten, 23-11 record
- Strong late-season form: Won BTT games vs Rutgers, MSU before losing to Purdue in final
- 5.5-point favorite, -235 moneyline

**UCF (10-seed)**
- KenPom #54, Torvik #58
- AdjEM: +15.05 (KenPom), ~+14.4 (Torvik)
- Good offense: AdjO 120.0-120.5 (39th-40th)
- Below-average defense: AdjD 105.5-105.7 (114th)
- Big 12, 21-11 record
- Some impressive wins (Kansas, Texas A&M, Texas Tech) but also some blowout losses
- Struggled late: lost to Oklahoma State and West Virginia before squeaking past Cincinnati in Big 12 tourney

The efficiency gap is significant (~6.5-8 points), UCLA has the better offense AND defense, and UCLA is favored by 5.5 points. The 7-10 matchup historically has about a 60-40 split favoring the 7-seed. UCF's Big 12 schedule gives them credibility, but their defense (114th) will struggle against UCLA's elite offense (17th-22nd). UCF's path to an upset would rely on their own solid offense keeping pace, but UCLA's defense is meaningfully better.

    "rationale": "UCLA holds a significant edge in both KenPom (#27 vs #54) and Torvik (#24 vs #58) rankings, with an adjusted efficiency margin roughly 6-8 points higher than UCF's, driven by advantages on both ends of the floor—particularly UCLA's elite offense (AdjO 123.8-124.5, 17th-22nd nationally) against UCF's below-average defense (AdjD 105.5-105.7, 114th nationally). The Bruins enter with strong momentum from their Big Ten Tournament run, beating Rutgers, Michigan State, and nearly toppling Purdue in the final, while UCF stumbled down the stretch with blowout losses to Oklahoma State and West Virginia before a narrow 66-65 escape against Cincinnati. While UCF's Big 12 pedigree and quality wins over Kansas and Texas Tech give them a credible upset path in this historically competitive 7-10 seed line, the 5.5-point spread and UCLA's clear statistical superiority on both sides of the ball make the Bruins the deserving favorite."
